column1: 2|Ikigai: The Japanese Secret to a Long and Happy Life|Book|According to the Japanese
column2: everyone has an ikigai—a reason for living. And according to the residents of the Japanese village with the world’s longest-living people
column3: finding it is the key to a happier and longer life. Having a strong sense of ikigai—where what you love
column5: what you can get paid for
column6: and what the world needs all overlap—means that each day is infused with meaning. It’s the reason we get up in the morning. It’s also the reason many Japanese never really retire (in fact there’s no word in Japanese that means retire in the sense it does in English): They remain active and work at what they enjoy
column7: because they’ve found a real purpose in life—the happiness of always being busy.  In researching this book
column8: the authors interviewed the residents of the Japanese village with the highest percentage of 100-year-olds—one of the world’s Blue Zones. Ikigai reveals the secrets to their longevity and happiness: how they eat
column11: how they foster collaboration and community
column12: and—their best-kept secret—how they find the ikigai that brings satisfaction to their lives. And it provides practical tools to help you discover your own ikigai. Because who doesn’t want to find happiness in every day?
